feed.title: Pronounce — recent dictionary additions
feed.subtitle: How engineers actually pronounce project / product / jargon names. Community-maintained.

entry.title: Blackwell — BLACK well
entry.summary: Blackwell is pronounced "BLACK well" (/ˈblæk wɛl/). "BLACK-well". NVIDIA B100/B200 architecture, named after mathematician David Blackwell.

entry.title: Lovelace — LUV lace
entry.summary: Lovelace is pronounced "LUV lace" (/ˈlʌv leɪs/). "LUV-lace" (RTX 40-series), after Ada Lovelace.

entry.title: Ampere — AM peer
entry.summary: Ampere is pronounced "AM peer" (/ˈæm pɪər/). US: "AM-peer"; UK: "AM-pair". RTX 30-series + A100.

entry.title: Volta — VOLE ta
entry.summary: Volta is pronounced "VOLE ta" (/ˈvoʊl tə/). "VOLE-ta". V100. Named after Alessandro Volta.

entry.title: Pascal — pas KAL
entry.summary: Pascal is pronounced "pas KAL" (/pæˈskæl/). Programming-language origin "PAS-kuhl"; NVIDIA uses "pas-KAL" in talks. After Blaise Pascal.

entry.title: Grace — grace
entry.summary: Grace is pronounced "grace" (/ɡreɪs/). "grace" the word. NVIDIA ARM CPU, named after Grace Hopper.

entry.title: Turing — TURE ing
entry.summary: Turing is pronounced "TURE ing" (/ˈtʊər ɪŋ/). "TURE-ing" (rhymes with "during"). RTX 20-series. After Alan Turing.

entry.title: Heartbleed — heart bleed
entry.summary: Heartbleed is pronounced "heart bleed" (/ˈhɑːrt bliːd/). "heart-bleed". CVE-2014-0160. OpenSSL bug.

entry.title: Shellshock — shell shock
entry.summary: Shellshock is pronounced "shell shock" (/ˈʃɛl ʃɒk/). "shell-shock". CVE-2014-6271. Bash bug.

entry.title: Log4Shell — log four shell
entry.summary: Log4Shell is pronounced "log four shell" (/ˌlɒɡ fɔːr ˈʃɛl/). "log-four-shell". CVE-2021-44228. log4j RCE.

entry.title: Spectre — SPEK ter
entry.summary: Spectre is pronounced "SPEK ter" (/ˈspɛk tər/). "SPEK-ter". CPU side-channel vuln (2018).

entry.title: Meltdown — melt down
entry.summary: Meltdown is pronounced "melt down" (/ˈmɛlt daʊn/). "melt-down". CPU vuln paired with Spectre.

entry.title: Druid — DROO id
entry.summary: Druid is pronounced "DROO id" (/ˈdruː ɪd/). "DROO-id". Real-time analytics DB.

entry.title: Pinot — PEE noh
entry.summary: Pinot is pronounced "PEE noh" (/ˈpiː noʊ/). "PEE-noh" (like the wine). Real-time OLAP store.

entry.title: TiDB — T I D B
entry.summary: TiDB is pronounced "T I D B" (/ˌtiː aɪ diː ˈbiː/). Letters "T-I-D-B". Hybrid SQL by PingCAP.

entry.title: YugabyteDB — YOO guh bite D B
entry.summary: YugabyteDB is pronounced "YOO guh bite D B" (/ˈjuː ɡə baɪt diː biː/). "YOO-guh-bite-D-B". Distributed SQL.

entry.title: StarRocks — star rocks
entry.summary: StarRocks is pronounced "star rocks" (/ˈstɑːr rɒks/). "star-rocks". OLAP database.

entry.title: Doris — DOR is
entry.summary: Doris is pronounced "DOR is" (/ˈdɔːr ɪs/). "DOR-is". MPP analytical DB.

entry.title: Hardhat — hard hat
entry.summary: Hardhat is pronounced "hard hat" (/ˈhɑːrd hæt/). "hard-hat". Ethereum development environment.

entry.title: Foundry — FOUN dree
entry.summary: Foundry is pronounced "FOUN dree" (/ˈfaʊn dri/). "FOUN-dree". Solidity smart contract toolkit.

entry.title: Anvil — AN vil
entry.summary: Anvil is pronounced "AN vil" (/ˈæn vɪl/). "AN-vil". Foundry local Ethereum node.

entry.title: Solana — suh LAH nuh
entry.summary: Solana is pronounced "suh LAH nuh" (/səˈlɑː nə/). "suh-LAH-nuh". L1 blockchain.

entry.title: WHATWG — WAT wig
entry.summary: WHATWG is pronounced "WAT wig" (/ˈwɒt wɪɡ/). "WHAT-wig" common; letter-by-letter also heard. Web Hypertext Application Technology Working Group.

entry.title: ECMA — EK ma
entry.summary: ECMA is pronounced "EK ma" (/ˈɛk mə/). "EK-ma" (one word) is the org's own usage; letter-by-letter also common. European Computer Manufacturers Association (legacy name).

entry.title: IETF — I E T F
entry.summary: IETF is pronounced "I E T F" (/ˌaɪ iː tiː ˈɛf/). Letters "I-E-T-F". Internet Engineering Task Force.
